text: The robotic-assisted hysterectomy begins with clamping, fulgurating, and cutting the round, broad, and utero-ovarian ligaments, ensuring hemostasis on both medial and lateral pedicles. The ovaries are preserved, falling gently away as the tissue is cut. The lower cardinal ligaments are transected, exposing the uterine vessels, and the right ureter is identified to avoid damage. The anterior bladder flap is dissected to access the vesicle uterine avascular space. The utero-sacral ligaments are transected, leading to entry into the posterior vagina. Finally, circumferential transection of the cervix from the vaginal cuff is completed.

The surgeon applies gentle pressure to deliver the uterus from the surgical field. The vaginal cuff is closed using figure-of-eight vicryl sutures through the mucosa, ensuring complete closure and hemostasis.
